review_priority.model: Review priority combines two ordinal ranks by ADDING their positions (a rank sum, not a product), then bands the sum into Critical/High/Medium/Low. Likelihood is an additive point score over archetype-weighted reachability (service-conditional), no-CMVP-validation-update, >=18mo staleness, and measured upstream CVE drift (scored at both >=10 and >=25, so drift weighs most). Impact is an expert prior per archetype. Every input is explicit and evidence-graded. These are attack-path REVIEW-ORDER CANDIDATES requiring confirmation, NOT confirmed vulnerabilities or a severity score.

components.note: Generic full-record component identification (module name + software/firmware versions + SP body/tables) against a CPE-mapped catalog. 'strong' = the module ships/names it in a name/version field; 'referenced' = mentioned in the SP body.

motifs.note: A motif is an architectural pattern where a known vulnerability CLASS would matter, matched from public signals (identified components, interfaces, services, archetype, SP keywords). A match means the corpus reveals the pattern — NOT that the module is vulnerable.

boot-chain verification.description: Bootloader / verified-boot / signed-image verification path is present.
boot-chain verification.external_anchor: Binarly U-Boot FIT signature-verification bypass (CVE-2026-46728, U-Boot < 2026.04)
boot-chain verification.can_cannot: can: the boot-integrity verification surface exists. cannot: exact bootloader version, whether the affected path is built in, exploitability.

firmware-update authentication.description: Firmware/image update path with signature/authenticity checking.
firmware-update authentication.can_cannot: can: an update-authentication path likely exists. cannot: whether the implementation is vulnerable.

network crypto parser/protocol.description: A named TLS/crypto library is consumed by a network service (TLS/SSH/IKE) — handshake / X.509 / ASN.1 parsing surface.
network crypto parser/protocol.can_cannot: can: named component has CVE pressure and a plausibly-consuming network service. cannot: whether the vulnerable path is reachable pre-auth.

debug/recovery interface.description: Local debug / recovery / update-media interface exposed (JTAG/UART/SPI/I2C/USB/DFU/recovery).
debug/recovery interface.can_cannot: can: a local/debug/update surface is listed. cannot: whether it is enabled in production.

kernel crypto consumer.description: Linux-kernel crypto exposed through consumers (IPsec, storage, VPN, TLS offload, filesystem crypto).
kernel crypto consumer.can_cannot: can: possible downstream kernel-crypto consumers. cannot: which subsystem is actually enabled/exposed.

HSM/SE firmware trust anchor.description: High-value HSM / secure-element trust boundary with a firmware/update path.
HSM/SE firmware trust anchor.external_anchor: HSM / secure-element firmware-lineage and update-path classes
HSM/SE firmware trust anchor.can_cannot: can: a high-impact firmware trust boundary exists. cannot: firmware lineage, patchability, production config.

throughput_predictors.note: Complexity proxies for REVIEW BURDEN — candidate predictors of validation effort. The corpus is survivorship-biased (validated modules only) and has NO pipeline-timing data (no IUT/Cost-Recovery/Pending-Review durations, no comment cycles), so these are HYPOTHESES, not measured time drivers.
not_determinable_without_MIP_snapshots: how long abandoned or still-pending modules have waited (not in a validated-cert corpus at all)

coverage.sweep_range: cert #4650–#5159 (near-census of FIPS 140-3 in this window)
coverage.fields_from_cert_page: level, type, embodiment, vendor, standard, status, validationHistory (dates/lab), sunset, approvedAlgorithms
coverage.fields_from_security_policy: sections, revisionHistory, ports/interfaces, services, SSPs, approvedAlgorithmsDetailed, tables
coverage.dedup_rule: one record per certificate number; cross-cert rebrand/re-validation chains NOT yet merged

families.note: Certificates clustered by normalized vendor + de-noised module name. A conservative, deterministic heuristic (no NIST 'replaced-by' fetch), so family and successor counts are lower bounds.
